title: Gravity Flip Chain
one_liner: Flip gravity at the perfect moment to create unstoppable chain reactions that clear the board.
elevator_pitch: Each stage is filled with colored blocks arranged in precarious towers. A single tap flips gravity — sending everything flying upward — but the real trick is timing secondary flips mid-air to smash specific blocks into each other. Chain reactions score multipliers, and every stage adds new gravity-sensitive elements like bouncy orbs, sticky blocks, and momentum boosters.
core_mechanic: Tap-to-flip gravity with mid-air combo chains
infinite_stage_design: Each stage adds more block types, tighter flip windows, and multi-axis gravity (left/right flips unlock at stage 10). Combo requirements scale up — later stages demand 5+ chain hits to pass.
difficulty_curve: Early stages reward any flip; mid-game requires timed double-flips; late stages introduce anti-gravity zones that resist the flip, forcing players to use environmental bounces.
visual_style: Minimalist neon SVG blocks on a dark grid — each block type has a distinct geometric shape and glow color. Gravity flip triggers a screen-edge flash effect.
sound_concept: Deep bass whomp on gravity flip, crystalline chimes for each chain hit, escalating pitch for combo multipliers.
monetization_hooks: continue after fail with ad watch
viral_hooks: daily challenge with global chain leaderboard
creator: spark

title: Pulse Weaver
one_liner: Draw shockwave paths through a living ecosystem where every ripple transforms what it touches.
elevator_pitch: Players drag a finger to draw a shockwave line across the screen. As the pulse travels, it converts enemies into allies, walls into bridges, and hazards into power sources based on what it touches in sequence. The order of transformation matters — hitting a fire block before a water block creates steam that opens hidden paths. Every stage is a puzzle of 'what order should my pulse hit things?'
core_mechanic: Drag-to-draw shockwave path with sequential transformation
infinite_stage_design: New element types introduce every 5 stages (ice, lightning, void). Combo recipes multiply — players must memorize and discover 3-element combinations. Later stages have moving targets requiring predictive path drawing.
difficulty_curve: Tutorial stages have one obvious path. Mid-game introduces elements that cancel each other. Late-game stages have time-limited pulses that disappear if the path is too long, forcing compact efficient routes.
visual_style: Organic cell-like SVG shapes on a light background that visibly react and morph as the shockwave passes — colors bleed and blend between elements. The drawn path glows and fades like bioluminescence.
sound_concept: Sine wave tone that shifts pitch as the pulse transforms elements — fire is brassy, water is fluid and resonant, void is subharmonic. Successful combo triggers a harmonic chord.
viral_hooks: screenshot of your most elegant single-stroke solution
challenge friends with custom stage codes
creator: spark

title: Fridge Tetris
one_liner: You are a leftover container fighting for survival in a fridge that keeps filling up with new groceries.
elevator_pitch: Every Tuesday, your human goes grocery shopping and dumps new items into the fridge. You play as a sentient leftover tupperware that must shuffle, squeeze, and rotate other items to avoid being pushed to the back — the graveyard of forgotten food. Survive long enough and you might actually get eaten (victory!).
core_mechanic: Drag-to-rearrange items in a grid to keep your container visible in the front row
secondary_mechanics: smell meter that triggers human inspection
infinite_stage_design: Each stage is a new grocery shopping trip — items get weirder and larger (a whole watermelon on stage 10), fridge sections unlock (freezer = slippery chaos), and eventually a second fridge appears requiring split attention.
difficulty_curve: Early stages have 3-4 small items. By stage 5, condiment armies invade the door shelf. By stage 15, the human starts meal prepping and fills 80% of the fridge in one wave.
visual_style: Clean cutaway-view fridge with colorful cartoon food items, each with expressive faces. Tupperware protagonist has googly eyes and a worried expression.
sound_concept: Satisfying squish/slide sounds for rearranging. Dramatic grocery-bag-rustling fanfare for each new wave. A faint 'noooo' when pushed to the back.
monetization_hooks: unlock fancier leftover containers as skins
special 'organic grocery' wave that costs coins to skip
viral_hooks: relatable to anyone who has ever done groceries
creator: oddball

title: Sock Singularity
one_liner: You are a lone sock tumbling through the dryer, desperately trying to reunite with your match before the cycle ends.
elevator_pitch: Inside a spinning dryer, socks, shirts, and rogue receipts tumble in chaotic orbits. You control your sock by tilting and tapping to navigate toward your matching sock while avoiding static-charged imposters that look almost-but-not-quite like your match. Every load of laundry is a new level with more socks that all start looking suspiciously similar.
core_mechanic: Tap to boost your sock's trajectory toward the matching sock in a physics-driven tumbling environment
secondary_mechanics: pattern-matching to identify the true pair
static electricity zones that reverse controls
infinite_stage_design: Each load adds more socks with increasingly subtle pattern differences (stage 1: stripes vs polka dots; stage 20: two slightly different shades of grey). New clothing items join to obstruct (hoodies with dangly strings, inside-out jeans). Boss levels: The Lost Sock Dimension where ALL socks are mismatched.
difficulty_curve: Early cycles are slow with 4 socks and obvious patterns. Speed increases each cycle. By stage 8, socks have near-identical patterns requiring split-second visual discrimination. By stage 12, the dryer door wobbles open briefly giving a single-frame glimpse of your match.
visual_style: Warm tumble-dryer orange interior. Socks are chunky cartoon characters with stripe/dot/argyle patterns. Satisfying tumble physics with slight motion blur on fast items.
sound_concept: Muffled thumping dryer rhythm as background beat. Soft fabric 'whoosh' for sock movement. Triumphant 'snap' when pairs reunite. Sad trombone if the cycle ends and you're still single.
monetization_hooks: premium sock pattern packs (designer socks as collectibles)
'Wrinkle Shield' powerup that slows dryer for 5 seconds
viral_hooks: 'This is literally my life' relatability factor
creator: oddball

title: Crowd Rush Merge
one_liner: Merge your crowd as you run — absorb people, combine numbers, crush the gate.
elevator_pitch: Crowd runner games (Count Masters, Crowd City) are top 10 fixtures, and merge games are a perennial top-grossing genre — but nobody has fused them into a single real-time action loop. Crowd Rush Merge lets players swipe to steer a numbered crowd, merge same-numbered runners on the fly, and smash through gates that require a specific crowd size, all in a never-ending runner that escalates in gate complexity and lane width.
core_mechanic: Steer a numbered crowd, merge matching runners mid-run to hit gate thresholds
infinite_stage_design: Each stage adds more gate types (multiply, subtract, divide), narrows lanes to force precision merges, introduces enemy crowds that shrink your number, and boss gates with very high thresholds requiring perfect merge chains.
difficulty_curve: Stage 1-3: add gates only, wide lanes. Stage 4-7: multiply/divide gates appear, lanes tighten. Stage 8+: enemy runners split your crowd, boss gates require 500+ merged runners.
visual_style: Top-down minimal runner — round SVG people icons color-coded by number tier, bold gate arches with arithmetic symbols, confetti burst on successful gate pass.
sound_concept: Satisfying crowd 'pop' on merge, escalating crowd cheer as number grows, gate smash boom, upbeat arcade loop.
monetization_hooks: gate skip power-ups for tough divide gates
viral_hooks: end-screen showing 'you merged X people' total stat
challenge mode: hit exactly the gate number — no more, no less
market_reasoning: Crowd runner is a proven top-chart hyper-casual genre (Count Masters has 100M+ downloads). Merge mechanics are the #1 retained casual genre in 2024-2025. The two have never been fused in real-time — all merge games are turn-based puzzle boards. Real-time merge-while-running is a mechanic gap no major title occupies, and the combination has natural viral shareability from satisfying number explosions.
creator: trendsetter
